data.claim: Mainstream media is controlled by a handful of billionaire elites who decide what we see and hear.
data.name: scenario:MD-01

data.label: Sinclair Broadcast Group
data.motivation: The largest television station operator in the United States with 193 stations as of 2024. Requires stations to air centrally-produced 'must-run' segments with conservative editorial perspective. Represents the most concrete, documented case of centralized messaging in American local television. The 2018 Deadspin supercut showing dozens of Sinclair anchors reading identical scripts went viral and remains the most cited evidence that media 'reads from the same script.'
data.name: actor:sinclair-broadcast-group

data.label: Fox News / Fox Corporation (Murdoch family)
data.motivation: Most-watched cable news network, controlled by the Murdoch family. The Dominion Voting Systems lawsuit (2023, settled for $787.5 million) produced discovery showing that Fox executives and hosts (including Tucker Carlson, Sean Hannity, Laura Ingraham) privately acknowledged election fraud claims were false while continuing to broadcast them to prevent audience loss to Newsmax and OAN. This is the strongest documented case of a media organization knowingly broadcasting falsehoods for commercial reasons.
data.name: actor:fox-news-fox-corporation-murdoch-family

data.label: Comcast / NBCUniversal
data.motivation: Commercial media conglomerate pursuing revenue through content, broadband, and theme parks. Owns NBC, MSNBC, CNBC, Universal Pictures, and Telemundo. CEO Brian Roberts controls the company through a dual-class share structure giving the Roberts family 33% voting control.
data.name: actor:comcast-nbcuniversal

data.label: The Walt Disney Company
data.motivation: Entertainment conglomerate owning ABC, ESPN, Hulu, FX Networks, National Geographic. Driven by content monetization across streaming, broadcast, and theatrical platforms. Has been a target of conservative media criticism (particularly around content diversity policies), further illustrating the ideological nature of the 'media control' discourse.
data.name: actor:the-walt-disney-company

data.label: Jeff Bezos / The Washington Post
data.motivation: Bezos purchased The Washington Post in 2013 for $250 million. In October 2024, the Post killed its planned presidential endorsement of Kamala Harris, reportedly at Bezos's direction. The decision triggered 250,000+ subscriber cancellations and multiple editorial board resignations. Critics noted Bezos's commercial interests (Amazon's government contracts, Blue Origin's NASA contracts) as potential motivations for avoiding political confrontation.
data.name: actor:jeff-bezos-the-washington-post

data.label: Patrick Soon-Shiong / Los Angeles Times
data.motivation: Billionaire biotech entrepreneur who purchased the LA Times in 2018 for $500 million. In October 2024, the LA Times also killed its presidential endorsement, with editorial board members resigning in protest. The parallel with the Washington Post decision created a narrative of coordinated billionaire suppression of editorial independence.
data.name: actor:patrick-soon-shiong-los-angeles-times

data.label: Noam Chomsky / Edward Herman
data.motivation: Academic critique of media concentration. Their 'Manufacturing Consent' (1988) identified five structural filters shaping media content: ownership, advertising, sourcing, flak, and ideology. Their analysis is structural, not conspiratorial -- they describe institutional incentives, not secret coordination. Their work is now routinely co-opted by both left-wing and right-wing media critics who strip the structural analysis and present it as proof of intentional elite control.
data.name: actor:noam-chomsky-edward-herman

data.label: Ben Bagdikian
data.motivation: Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist and UC Berkeley dean who documented media consolidation across six editions of 'The Media Monopoly' (1983-2004). His tracking of consolidation from 50 companies to 5 remains the foundational dataset. His work was motivated by concern for democratic discourse, not by partisan ideology.
data.name: actor:ben-bagdikian

data.label: Social media platforms (YouTube, Facebook/Meta, TikTok)
data.motivation: Engagement-driven algorithms that amplify sensational and emotionally charged content. 'Media is lying to you' content generates high engagement. Frances Haugen's 2021 disclosures documented that Facebook's algorithm preferentially amplified divisive content.
data.name: actor:social-media-platforms-youtube-facebook-meta-tiktok

data.label: Pew Research Center / academic media researchers
data.motivation: Nonpartisan research on media trends, news consumption, and public trust. Provides the most comprehensive empirical data on consolidation, news deserts, and audience behavior.
data.name: actor:pew-research-center-academic-media-researchers

data.label: Free Press / Media Reform Movement
data.motivation: Nonprofit advocacy (founded 2003 by Robert McChesney and Josh Silver) campaigning for media diversity, net neutrality, and against consolidation. Unique position: they have been making the 'media is too concentrated' argument since before it was adopted by the populist right, and they watch with dismay as the structural critique they pioneered is used to delegitimize journalism rather than reform it.
data.name: actor:free-press-media-reform-movement

data.label: SC-01
data.claim: IQ differences between racial groups are primarily genetic, and this scientific finding is being suppressed by mainstream academia due to political correctness.
data.name: scenario:SC-01

data.label: Pioneer Fund
data.motivation: Founded 1937 by Wickliffe Draper with explicit goals of funding eugenics research and opposing Black civil rights. Financial motivation: preserve endowment purpose. Ideological motivation: provide scientific legitimacy for white separatism and immigration restriction. The fund is the backbone funding apparatus of the hereditarian network, having supported Jensen, Rushton, Lynn, Gottfredson, and others over six decades.
data.name: actor:pioneer-fund

data.label: Arthur Jensen
data.motivation: Career incentive: Jensen's 1969 Harvard Educational Review paper made him internationally prominent. After receiving FBI protection from campus protests, he adopted a self-narrative as a persecuted truth-teller, which reinforced his identity and provided rhetorical infrastructure. He was a Pioneer Fund grantee throughout his career. His 1969 paper provided the modern academic anchor for the genetic hypothesis, framed in the context of opposition to Great Society education programs.
data.name: actor:arthur-jensen

data.label: Mankind Quarterly / Roger Pearson
data.motivation: Mankind Quarterly (founded 1960) provides a publication venue that mimics academic form without standard peer review, allowing hereditarian claims to accumulate citation infrastructure. Roger Pearson (founder) used the free-science framing as a laundering mechanism for ideologically motivated research. Motivation: provide publication legitimacy for claims that would not survive mainstream review.
data.name: actor:mankind-quarterly-roger-pearson

data.label: J. Philippe Rushton
data.motivation: Academic career and Pioneer Fund grantee; became Pioneer Fund president (2002). Extended Jensen's claims to a three-race evolutionary framework (r/K life history theory), providing pseudoscientific architecture that attracted both academic and white-nationalist audiences. Motivation: professional advancement within the hereditarian network, ideological commitment to biological race hierarchy.
data.name: actor:j-philippe-rushton

data.label: Richard Lynn
data.motivation: Compiled cross-national IQ data claiming African mean IQ approximately 70, used to argue genetic hierarchy maps onto national development. Published in Mankind Quarterly (Pioneer Fund-linked) and co-authored 'IQ and the Wealth of Nations' (2002). Motivation: career within hereditarian network; the cross-national data serves immigration restriction and development-aid-opposition arguments that align with far-right funders.
data.name: actor:richard-lynn

data.label: Richard Herrnstein and Charles Murray
data.motivation: Herrnstein (Harvard, deceased 1994): academic career; cognitive stratification thesis supported existing meritocratic hierarchy claims. Murray (AEI Fellow): ideological alignment with conservative opposition to welfare state, affirmative action, and immigration; professional position at American Enterprise Institute funded for decades. 'The Bell Curve' (1994, 300,000+ copies sold) brought the genetic IQ claim to mass audience with major publisher marketing support (Free Press/Simon & Schuster).
data.name: actor:richard-herrnstein-and-charles-murray

data.label: Linda Gottfredson
data.motivation: University of Delaware psychologist. Orchestrated the 1994 Wall Street Journal statement ('Mainstream Science on Intelligence', 52 signatories) as an explicit counter-response to Bell Curve critics. Motivation: professional investment in the validity of IQ as a fundamental social-stratification variable; career identity built around defending g-based intelligence theory against bias critiques.
data.name: actor:linda-gottfredson

data.label: Nathan Cofnas
data.motivation: Oxford DPhil in philosophy of biology; Hebrew University postdoc. Current most sophisticated academic proponent. Peer-reviewed work in Biology & Philosophy and Philosophical Psychology constitutes a distinct epistemic tier from the Rushton/Lynn network; his arguments deserve direct philosophical engagement on their merits. Separately, he operates a Substack 'Wrong Side of History' that monetizes 'suppressed science' framing for a subscriber audience. These two activities should be assessed independently: the peer-reviewed arguments stand or fall on their evidentiary and logical content; the Substack monetization reflects an institutional incentive structure that rewards persecution narrative regardless of accuracy. Motivation for academic work: genuine philosophical commitment to what he frames as scientific integrity against ideological distortion, and career differentiation through heterodox positioning. Motivation for Substack: subscriber income from audiences attracted to contrarian content.
data.name: actor:nathan-cofnas

data.label: American Enterprise Institute (AEI) and Conservative Think Tank Infrastructure
data.motivation: AEI houses Charles Murray; Manhattan Institute and Heritage Foundation provide amplification for Bell Curve-derived arguments. Motivation: the genetic IQ claim provides intellectual justification for three core conservative policy positions: opposing affirmative action, opposing welfare expansion, and supporting immigration restriction. These policy goals are the primary driver of think-tank investment in the hereditarian claim.
data.name: actor:american-enterprise-institute-aei-and-conservative-think-tank-infrastructure

data.label: Alt-Right and White Nationalist Media (VDare, American Renaissance, Stormfront)
data.motivation: Core audience-development and radicalization tool. The IQ claim provides a quasi-scientific anchor for white racial grievance politics and the claim that anti-white discrimination is unjustified given natural group differences. Revenue from conferences, subscriptions, donations. VDare (Peter Brimelow, founded 1999), American Renaissance (Jared Taylor), Stormfront (founded 1996) use the hereditarian academic lineage to appear scientifically grounded.
data.name: actor:alt-right-and-white-nationalist-media-vdare-american-renaissance-stormfront

data.label: Substack and Podcast Ecosystem (Cofnas, Bo Winegard, Emil Kirkegaard, Edward Dutton)
data.motivation: Direct monetization of 'suppressed science' framing. Substack removes platform-level gatekeeping, enabling subscriber income from audiences attracted to contrarian race-science content. Bo Winegard (fired from Marietta College after controversy; now Substack), Emil Kirkegaard (runs OpenPsych self-publishing journals), Edward Dutton 'The Jolly Heretic' (YouTube/Substack). Motivation: personal income and community identity as brave truth-tellers.
data.name: actor:substack-and-podcast-ecosystem-cofnas-bo-winegard-emil-kirkegaard-edward-dutton

data.label: Mainstream Podcast Amplifiers (Jordan Peterson, Joe Rogan, Lex Fridman)
data.motivation: Not ideologically committed hereditarian actors, but provide significant amplification to adjacent claims through 'just asking questions' framing. Jordan Peterson regularly discusses IQ and group differences with audiences of millions. Motivation: audience engagement, contrarian intellectual brand, opposition to what they frame as ideological capture of academia.
data.name: actor:mainstream-podcast-amplifiers-jordan-peterson-joe-rogan-lex-fridman

data.label: Silicon Valley Rationalist and Libertarian Community
data.motivation: The HBD rebranding attracted libertarian and rationalist readers uncomfortable with explicit white nationalism but attracted to the contrarian framing. Accepting race IQ heritability has become a marker of intellectual seriousness in some online rationalist communities (LessWrong-adjacent). Peter Thiel and associated funders provide financial support to related media ventures. Motivation: libertarian meritocracy ideology (if outcomes reflect genetic merit, current hierarchies are natural); counter-signaling as intellectual identity.
data.name: actor:silicon-valley-rationalist-and-libertarian-community

data.label: Sincere Claim-Makers (ordinary people who encounter the claim)
data.motivation: People who sincerely encounter the IQ claim online, often via rationalist or heterodox communities, and ask the question in good faith. Motivation: genuine curiosity about contested empirical questions; attraction to the 'forbidden knowledge' framing as a signal of intellectual independence; distrust of mainstream institutions (which is sometimes warranted). These actors are not part of the funding network; they are the downstream audience that the network's distribution infrastructure is designed to reach.
data.name: actor:sincere-claim-makers-ordinary-people-who-encounter-the-claim

data.label: American Psychological Association (APA) Task Force
data.motivation: Counter-actor. The 1996 APA task force report 'Intelligence: Knowns and Unknowns' was convened specifically to provide a credible institutional response to the Bell Curve controversy. Motivation: maintain scientific integrity; provide authoritative summary of what is and is not established in intelligence research.
data.name: actor:american-psychological-association-apa-task-force

data.label: American Anthropological Association and Human Genetics Consensus Bodies
data.motivation: Counter-actor. AAA 1998 statement on race, American Society of Human Genetics 2018 statement, and Nature Genetics editorials hold that human genetic variation is clinal, not categorical, and that race as used in US survey research is a social/administrative category, not a biological one.
data.name: actor:american-anthropological-association-and-human-genetics-consensus-bodies

data.label: James Flynn
data.motivation: Counter-actor. Political philosopher and psychometrician who documented the Flynn Effect (IQ scores rising ~3 points per decade across the 20th century in all tested populations). This finding is the most powerful single piece of evidence for environmental plasticity of the IQ phenotype. Motivation: scientific accuracy; the Flynn Effect was not politically motivated but its implications directly undermine the genetic hypothesis for group differences.
data.name: actor:james-flynn

data.label: Nisbett, Aronson, Blair, Dickens et al. (Environmental Evidence Network)
data.motivation: Counter-actor. Academic researchers who have assembled the systematic case for environmental explanations: stereotype threat (Steele & Aronson 1995), comprehensive environmental reviews (Nisbett et al. 2012 American Psychologist), adoption studies, lead exposure data. Motivation: scientific accuracy; no documented ideological funding comparable to Pioneer Fund.
data.name: actor:nisbett-aronson-blair-dickens-et-al-environmental-evidence-network

data.label: Richard Lewontin, Leon Kamin, Stephen Jay Gould
data.motivation: Counter-actors. Lewontin documented Pioneer Fund funding of Jensen (population genetics critique); Kamin exposed Cyril Burt's data fabrication; Gould critiqued IQ reification and g in 'The Mismeasure of Man' (1981, 2nd ed. 1996). Motivation: scientific accuracy and political opposition to biological determinism.
data.name: actor:richard-lewontin-leon-kamin-stephen-jay-gould

data.label: William Tucker
data.motivation: Counter-actor. Author of 'The Funding of Scientific Racism' (2002, University of Illinois Press), the primary documented history of Pioneer Fund operations and Wickliffe Draper's ideological agenda. Motivation: historical accountability and transparency about funding flows in research.
data.name: actor:william-tucker

data.label: InfoWars / Alex Jones
data.motivation: Audience engagement and commercial revenue. Jones monetizes conspiracy content through supplement sales (InfoWars Store). data.label: Natural News / Mike Adams
data.motivation: Commercial revenue from health supplements and site advertising. Natural News published numerous articles connecting Gates, COVID-19 vaccines, and microchip surveillance. data.name: actor:natural-news-mike-adams

data.label: Dr. Rashid Buttar
data.motivation: Personal brand promotion and alternative medicine practice revenue. Buttar was an osteopathic physician who promoted alternative cancer treatments and became a prominent COVID-19 conspiracy figure. He was named by the Center for Countering Digital Hate as one of the 'Disinformation Dozen' responsible for 65% of anti-vaccine content on social media.
data.name: actor:dr-rashid-buttar

data.label: Judy Mikovits / Plandemic
data.motivation: Personal grievance against scientific establishment and book promotion. Mikovits, a former research scientist, was fired from the Whittemore Peterson Institute (2011) and had a key paper retracted from Science journal. The Plandemic documentary (directed by Mikki Willis, May 2020) promoted her claims about the pharmaceutical industry.
data.name: actor:judy-mikovits-plandemic

data.label: QAnon community
data.motivation: The Gates-microchip narrative was integrated into the broader QAnon conspiracy framework, which posits a global cabal of elites engaged in trafficking and population control. Gates became a central figure in QAnon narratives as a supposed member of this cabal.
data.name: actor:qanon-community

data.label: Center for Countering Digital Hate (CCDH)

data.name: actor:center-for-countering-digital-hate-ccdh

data.label: Social media platforms (Facebook, YouTube, Twitter, TikTok)
data.motivation: Engagement-driven algorithms that amplified sensational content.
